0.q: the proton electrochemical gradient across the vacuolar membranes of transgenic cells was higher than that of WT cells...This would provide more motive force for the V-Na+/H+ antiporter to sequester Na+ into vacuoles.
TsVP transgenic tobacco plants accumulated 20–32% more Na+ under salt stress conditions... compartmentalization of Na+ in vacuoles reduces its toxic effects on plant cells.
overexpression of H+-PPase causes the accumulation of Na+ and Cl- in vacuoles instead of in the cytoplasm, thus reducing their toxic effects.

2.q: A number of genes encoding enzymes involved in phosphoinositide (PI) and inositol polyphosphate pathways have been isolated and biochemically characterized.

3.q: TsNAC1 negatively regulates the expansion of cells, inhibits LIGHT-DEPENDENT SHORT HYPOCOTYLS1 and UDP-XYLOSYLTRANSFERASE2

5.q: TsNAC1 was shown to target the positive regulators of ion transportation, such as T. HALOPHILA H+-PPASE1

6.q: maintained the balance of osmotic potential by modulating the ratio of Na+/K+ in plant cells... up-regulations of the osmotic stress-related genes such as the cation/H+ antiporter gene and the vacuolar cation/proton exchanger 1 gene (CAX1)

7.q: Protein interaction showed that the ThPP1 protein specifically interacted with a 16# target partner of the photosystem II light-harvesting-Chl-binding protein.
